scuba-diving.description: Tubbataha for the wall dives, Anilao for the macro shots, Coron for the wartime wrecks. The country basically wrote the textbook.

freediving.description: Panglao and Moalboal for the sardine run, Romblon for clear water depth lines. One breath, no tank, mas tahimik.

snorkeling-island-hopping.description: El Nido and Coron for the lagoons, Honda Bay for the sandbars, Moalboal if you want the sardines without a tank. Standard bangka day.

canyoneering.description: Kawasan in Badian is the standard route, three to four hours of jumps and slides ending at the falls. Bring water shoes, ang gaspang.

surfing.description: Cloud 9 in Siargao is the famous one, Baler is where most learn, La Union handles the off-season crowd.

whitewater-rafting.description: Cagayan de Oro is the home base, beginner sections and pro sections on the same river. Helmet on, paddle, listen to the guide.

wakeboarding.description: CamSur Watersports Complex in Pili is the country's wakeboard mecca, full cable park setup. Decathlon Nuvali na lang sa Manila side.

kiteboarding.description: Bulabog Beach in Boracay is the main spot, Caticlan and Cuyo also pick up during amihan season. Wind months are November to April.

sea-kayaking.description: El Nido's Big Lagoon and Small Lagoon are the postcard runs, Coron's Twin Lagoon is the other one. Paddle in, swim, paddle out.

sup-paddleboarding.description: Calm bays make it work, so try Anilao, Puerto Galera, or the back side of Boracay early morning before the wind picks up.

jet-skiing.description: Boracay, Mactan, and Subic for the rental booths along the beach. Banana boat goes with it, usually same operator, same bangka.

flyboarding.description: Boracay and Mactan have the operators with the trained spotters. Strap the boots, point your toes, the water pressure does the rest.

parasailing.description: Boracay White Beach is the main one, Cebu's Mactan and Panglao also run it. You sit in a harness, the boat does the work.

river-trekking.description: Tinipak River in Tanay and Daraitan in Rizal are the day trip options from Manila, Cebu and Bohol have shorter ones tied to waterfall walks.

skimboarding.description: Liw-Liwa in Zambales is the unofficial home of Philippine skim, the beach break is short and the run-up is flat. La Union too.

paraw-sailing.description: Boracay sunset paraw is the classic, the outrigger sailboat with the colored sail. Cebu and Iloilo also run them, mas quiet sa boats.

whale-shark-watching.description: Snorkel beside butanding in Donsol Sorsogon from November to May, where boats follow gentle giants in the open sea instead of feeding them like in Oslob Cebu.

sardine-run.description: Just wade out from Panagsama Beach in Moalboal Cebu and you are already inside the sardine tornado, no boat ride needed, swirling silver all around you.

dolphin-whale-watching.description: Pamilacan Island off Baclayon Bohol runs early-morning boat trips where spinner dolphins surface in pods, and Bais City Negros Oriental has bigger sperm whale sightings.

hot-spring-bathing.description: Soak in a wood-fired kawa cauldron tub in Tibiao Antique, or hit Ardent Hot Spring at the foot of Hibok-Hibok in Camiguin where four cascading pools run around 40 degrees.

waterfall-jumping.description: Kawasan Falls in Badian Cebu is the canyoneering circuit ending in three turquoise tiers, while Tinago Falls in Iligan and Aliwagwag in Cateel Davao Oriental hide their own jump spots.

bioluminescent-plankton.description: Paddle into Tay-tay Bay in El Nido or the Iwahig River in Puerto Princesa on a moonless night and every stroke lights up the water like blue glitter.

subwing.description: Get towed underwater by a banca off Station 1 Boracay holding a small wing board, banking left and right like a stingray a few meters under the surface.

bamboo-rafting.description: Glide down the green Loboc River in Bohol on a motor-pushed bamboo raft, or kayak-paddle one through Tibiao River in Antique past short rapids and quiet pools.

river-tubing.description: Drop into a rubber donut at Macahambus or Mapawa in Cagayan de Oro and ride the cool Cagayan River through short rapids, or do the same thing on Tibiao River in Antique.

deep-sea-fishing.description: Charter a boat out of Subic Bay Zambales for sailfish and dorado, or troll the deeper channels off Mactan Cebu and Camiguin where wahoo and tuna show up between March and June.

helmet-diving.description: Walk three meters down on the reef off Station 1 Boracay or in front of Mactan resorts wearing an air-fed helmet, no swimming skill needed, just step down the ladder.

e-foiling.description: Take an e-foil lesson in San Juan La Union or off Station 1 Boracay and after one or two hours of practice you are silently flying a foot above the surface.

underwater-scooter.description: Strap into a Scuba-doo bubble dome off Boracay and pilot yourself along the reef on a battery-powered scooter, breathing dry inside the helmet the whole time.

mermaid-swimming.description: Strap on a monofin and take a half-day mermaid class at Bulabog Beach in Boracay or Mactan Cebu where instructors teach the dolphin kick in shallow lagoons.

firefly-cruising.description: Drift down the Donsol River in Sorsogon or Iwahig River in Puerto Princesa Palawan after sundown on a paddle boat and watch mangrove trees flicker with thousands of fireflies.

hiking-mountaineering.description: Pulag para sa sea of clouds at sunrise, Apo for the slow alpine grind, Batulao kapag isang Sabado lang ang luwag mo.

volcano-trekking.description: Pinatubo crater for the turquoise lake payoff, Mayon for the perfect cone view, Taal kung gusto mo ng half-day climb from Tagaytay.

atv-offroading.description: Mayon ATV trail outside Legazpi crosses lahar fields right up to the 2006 mudflow wall, Pinatubo 4x4 from Santa Juliana hits Crow Valley before the crater hike.

spelunking.description: Sumaguing Cave in Sagada for the rimstone pools and barefoot scramble, Lumiang-Sumaguing connection for the 4-hour version, Sohoton in Samar kapag taga-Visayas ka.

sandboarding.description: La Paz Sand Dunes sa labas ng Laoag is solo siya, kasama na sa Paoay 4x4 tour ang board rental at ang lift back up after every run.

rock-climbing.description: Cantabaco sa Toledo, Cebu is the country's limestone mecca, Wawa Dam sa Rizal is where Manila climbers spend their Sundays, Atimonan crag for the Quezon coast crew.

mountain-biking.description: Timberland Hills sa Rizal for after-work loops, Sagada for the cold-air singletrack, Tagaytay ridge if you want espresso at every turn.

ziplining.description: Lake Sebu Seven Falls zipline soars 200 meters above the cascades in South Cotabato, Bohol and Subic round out the top three pero walang papantay sa height ng Lake Sebu.

camping-glamping.description: Treasure Mountain sa Tanay for the easy weekend pitch, Anawangin Cove for that pine-tree-by-the-sea shot, Caramoan if you want island-hopping plus tent.

survival-bushcraft.description: Subic jungle survival weekends are run by ex-military types, Palawan camps stretch the same idea over five days kasama ang foraging at firecraft.

dirt-biking.description: Mindoro for the open ranch trails and river crossings, Subic for the more organized motocross weekends.

horseback-riding.description: Wright Park in Baguio is the classic with its pony boys and rented riding gear, Tagaytay and Boracay round out the casual one-hour-ride circuit.

canopy-walk.description: Bohol Tree Top Adventure sa Loboc strings cables through old hardwood, Eden Nature Park sa Davao adds the rolling pine-mountain view.

zorbing.description: Sagada Zorbing sa Aguid is pretty much the only spot in the country, lower the ball down a grass hill outside town tapos uli sa van pabalik.

bungee-jumping.description: Danao Adventure Park sa Bohol runs The Plunge canyon swing 200 meters over the river, Plantation Bay sa Mactan keeps the more controlled resort version.

rappelling.description: Kawasan canyoneering sa Badian is the busiest with its waterfall jump-and-rappel combo, Talicud at Cantabaco serve the more technical climbing crowd.

wildlife-safari.description: Calauit Safari Park sa Busuanga still has the original African transplants from the 70s, Bohol Tarsier Sanctuary in Corella is the small endemic counterpart.

longboarding.description: Baguio crews bomb Kennon Road and the Marcos Highway descents, Tagaytay-Nasugbu is the second favorite kasi mas malapit sa Manila.

bird-watching.description: Olango Island sanctuary off Cebu fills up with migratory waders October to March, Candaba swamp sa Pampanga is the dry-season ducks-and-egrets spot, Palawan hides the endemic forest species.

trail-running.description: Tanay 50 in Rizal is the gateway ultra for Manila crews, King of the Mountain Marathon in Baguio is the elevation-heavy classic, mostly weekend warriors on Pulag and Cordillera trails sa labas ng race day.

paragliding.description: Carmona Ridge in Cavite gets you in the air less than an hour from Manila, and Sibulan in Negros Oriental opens up after the rainy season. Tandem flights only, no license needed.

skydiving.description: Skydive Greater Cebu drops you over Bantayan Island from 10,000 feet, sandbars and reef the whole way down. The Clark dropzone runs out of Pampanga for the Luzon side.

ultralight-flying.description: Angeles City Flying Club at Woodland Airpark in Magalang puts you in a two-seat ultralight with Mt. Arayat in the window. Open every day except Wednesday.

hot-air-ballooning.description: The Lubao International Balloon and Music Festival in Pampanga sends a few dozen balloons up at sunrise every February or March. Worth the early call time and the Pradera Verde drive.

helicopter-tours.description: Aerial loops over Makati, BGC, and Manila Bay launch out of the city heliports, and Cebu and Bohol operators run shorter routes over the islands. Book a sunset slot if the budget allows.

paramotoring.description: Subic Bay and San Felipe in Zambales are the usual tandem paramotor sites, and Carmona runs powered launches when the wind cooperates. Beach takeoffs are the easiest first ride.

gyrocopter-flying.description: Fly A Gyro Cebu runs short hops out of Ronda over Moalboal, Badian, and the southern Cebu coast, and Mactan handles intro flights closer to the airport.

sky-walk.description: The Sky Experience Adventure on top of Crown Regency Tower in Cebu City runs the Skywalk Extreme and the Edge Coaster 37 floors up. Closed shoes only, no flip-flops.

casino-gaming.description: Solaire, Okada, City of Dreams, and Newport line up along Entertainment City in Pasay and Parañaque. Pick by the headline show, the buffet, or just the lobby aircon on a hot afternoon.

theme-parks.description: Enchanted Kingdom in Sta. Rosa runs the Space Shuttle coaster and the Wheel of Fate every weekend, Star City in Pasay reopens after refits, and Inflatable Island floats off Subic in summer.

water-parks.description: Splash Island in Biñan runs the slide tower for the Laguna side, 8 Waves in Bulacan does wave pools for the north, and Anvaya Cove handles the resort-style swim in Bataan.

escape-rooms.description: Mystery Manila runs the BGC and Cebu IT Park branches with one-hour themed lockups. Group of four or five works best, six gets crowded in the smaller rooms.

interactive-museums.description: Art in Island in Cubao started the 3D selfie museum trend in Manila, Upside Down Museum in BGC keeps it going, and DreamPlay at City of Dreams Pasay leans full DreamWorks for the kids.

go-karting.description: City Kart Racing in Carmona Cavite is the closest track to Manila, and R1 Karting in the Subic Freeport runs the longer outdoor course. Helmets and overalls supplied.

paintball-airsoft.description: Sandbox at Alviera in Porac Pampanga keeps a paintball field next to the obstacle course, and FastBreak in Marikina runs airsoft skirmish weekends. Bring extra socks.

target-shooting.description: Armscor Shooting Center in Marikina is the long-running indoor range, and PNTC in Pasay handles the bigger lanes. Walk-in rentals are standard, IDs at the door.

bowling-billiards.description: SM Megamall and SM North run the cleaner mall bowling lanes, Star Mall Mandaluyong keeps the old-school setup, and the BGC pool halls stay open late for billiards.

karaoke-ktv.description: Music 21, Red Box, and Centerstage handle the private KTV room circuit across Metro Manila. Two-hour minimum on weekends, four-hour packages on Friday nights.

nightclubbing.description: Poblacion in Makati runs the bar-hop loop from Don Pedro to Felipe, BGC Forbestown keeps the late-night clubs, and Cubao X stays the indie holdout in Quezon City.

live-music.description: 70s Bistro in Quezon City and Mow's near UP keep the OPM gig circuit alive, Cohen's in BGC runs the jazz nights, and the Mall of Asia Arena handles the big-tour stops.

heritage-walking-tours.description: Intramuros in Manila has Casa Manila, Fort Santiago, and San Agustin Church on one walk, then Calle Crisologo in Vigan for a kalesa ride at sundown.

bambike-tours.description: Bambike Ecotours runs out of Plaza San Luis beside Casa Manila, so the guide rolls you through Fort Santiago, San Agustin, and Manila Cathedral on a bamboo bike, tara.

cultural-shows.description: Catch a Cordillera dance set at Panagbenga in Baguio, a T'boli performance by the lake huts in Lake Sebu, or a Bayanihan show during a Manila visit.

theater-musicals.description: CCP in Pasay still runs the Philippine Madrigal Singers and full musical seasons, PETA Theater in QC stages the Filipino originals, and Newport has Resorts World shows.

museum-art-tours.description: National Museum on Padre Burgos is free entry and holds the Spoliarium, then Ayala Museum in Makati has the diorama wing, and Pinto Art in Antipolo is a half-day on its own.

weaving-pottery.description: T'nalak weavers in Lake Sebu, Inabel looms in Vigan and Ilocos, and the Burnay potters along Liberation Boulevard still spin jars the kababayan way.

mambabatok-tattoos.description: Buscalan in Kalinga is where you go for an Apo Whang-od piece, or one from her grand-nieces Grace and Elyang. Plan it as a 2-day grind, the village is up a hike.

indigenous-games.description: Sagada lolos still carve the wooden trotrosiklo for the kids, and at Cordillera festivals you will see bamboo stilts and tug-of-war between barangays.

visita-iglesia.description: The Manaoag Basilica in Pangasinan, the Antipolo Cathedral on a Saturday vigil, and the seven Bohol baroque churches if you have a weekend in the Visayas.

sabong.description: Provincial cockpits draw the biggest crowds on Sunday derby days, with Smart Araneta Coliseum hosting the World Slasher Cup twice a year for the serious sabungero.

kawa-bath.description: Tibiao in Antique is the original kawa hot bath spot, the big cauldron set over firewood with calamansi leaves and flowers floating on top, sulit pagkatapos ng hike.

spa-wellness.description: The Farm at San Benito in Lipa is the splurge week, Nurture Wellness in Tagaytay is the easy weekend, and Mind Body Soul in Boracay does an open-air massage by the beach.

mega-mall-shopping.description: SM Mall of Asia in Pasay for the bayside walk, SM Megamall in Ortigas for the air-con marathon, then Greenbelt and BGC High Street for the slow afternoon.

food-tours.description: Big Binondo Food Wok with Ivan Man Dy starts at Eng Bee Tin, Pampanga Heritage Food Tour hits Aling Lucing's sisig, then Silay for the kabayanihan kakanin.

brewery-tours.description: Engkanto and Crows Brewing both run taproom visits in Metro Manila, San Miguel keeps the Mandaue brewery in Cebu on the tour circuit, and Tanduay does Cabuyao.

agritourism.description: Costales Nature Farms in Majayjay does the lechon-organic lunch, Gourmet Farms in Tagaytay sells the pesto fresh from the garden, and Yoki's Farm in Mendez has the pomelo pick.

staycations.description: Shangri-La Boracay on Punta Bunga, El Nido Resorts on the four private islands, Amanpulo if the budget allows, and Discovery Shores when you want the strip.

sunset-cruises.description: Manila Bay does the catamaran sunset off CCP, Boracay puts you on a paraw for the postcard shot, and Subic Bay has the catamaran from the Yacht Club.

golfing.description: Manila Southwoods in Carmona for the championship layout, Tagaytay Highlands for the cool air and Taal view, and Wack Wack in Mandaluyong if you can score a guest pass.

oceanariums.description: Manila Ocean Park behind Quirino Grandstand has the underwater tunnel, Sea World in Lapu-Lapu Cebu is the newer one, and Davao Crocodile Park bundles the croc show too.
